title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Runny Nose
intro: Herbal teas for Runny nose are a type of herbal remedy made from various plants, flowers, and herbs that are used to alleviate and treat nasal congestion, sinus pressure, and excessive mucus production. These teas work by soothing and calming the nasal passages, reducing inflammation, and promoting drainage of excess mucus, thereby providing relief from the discomfort and inconvenience of a runny nose. Some examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat runny nose include peppermint tea, which helps to open up airways and reduce congestion, ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the nasal passages, eucalyptus tea, which has decongestant properties that help to clear mucus and sinus pressure, and thyme tea, which has antimicrobial properties that help to combat infections that can cause runny nose. Additionally, other herbal teas such as elderflower, yarrow, and licorice root can also be used to treat runny nose, as they have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to reduce congestion and promote drainage.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat the runny nose ailment due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing nasal congestion, relieving sinus pressure, and promoting a clear respiratory tract. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus globulus teas, including eucalyptol, alpha-pinene, and beta-pinene, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of mucus and reducing inflammation in the nasal passages. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus teas can provide relief from runny nose symptoms and promote overall respiratory health, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and flu.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Measure 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) and set aside.
Add the Eucalyptus leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add honey (if using) and stir well before drinking.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the African journal of traditional, complementary, and alternative medicines : AJTCAM, Eucalyptus globulus teas may be used for runny nose, as part of traditional management of asthma in the Togolese traditional medicine.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can help alleviate a runny nose, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. When using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat a runny nose, it's essential to take precautions, such as starting with small doses, avoiding excessive consumption, and being cautious if you have sensitive stomachs or allergies.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea 3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves per cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to release essential oils.
Drink tea hot or warm, not cold.
Continue for 3-5 days for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat the runny nose ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and flavonoids, help to modulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating symptoms of a runny nose. The benefits of consuming Echinacea purpurea teas include reduced nasal congestion, decreased production of mucus, and a shorter duration of illness. Regular consumption of Echinacea purpurea teas may also help to boost the immune system and provide long-term protection against respiratory infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Echinacea purpurea flowers, stems, and leaves.
Combine the Echinacea mixture with 1 cup of boiling water in a pot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to make it sweeter.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve a runny nose.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Cochrane database of systematic reviews", Echinacea purpurea teas may be better than a placebo for relieving runny nose, although there is not enough evidence to recommend a specific product.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can help alleviate symptoms of a runny nose, but they may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using Echinacea purpurea teas include starting with a low dose to assess tolerance, avoiding it with other immunomodulatory herbs, and being mindful of potential interactions with medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 3 times a day.
Start with low dosage of 1 teaspoon per cup and adjust as needed.
Use 2-3 cups per day for 5-7 days to relieve runny nose symptoms.
Take breaks for 2-3 days and restart if symptoms persist.
Consult a healthcare professional for prolonged use or severe symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas, also known as elderflower tea, have been traditionally used to treat the runny nose ailment due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sambucus nigra, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sambunigrin, help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure, making it easier to breathe. By reducing inflammation and promoting the clearance of mucus, Sambucus nigra teas provide relief from the symptoms of a runny nose, such as congestion, sneezing, and postnasal drip. The benefits of using Sambucus nigra teas to treat a runny nose include natural and non-invasive relief, reduced risk of side effects, and the potential to boost the immune system with regular consumption.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Sambucus nigra flowers or 2-3 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add the Sambucus nigra flowers to the boiling water and reduce heat to low.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help relieve a runny nose.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Turkish archives of otorhinolaryngology", Sambucus nigra teas may be a beneficial and safe option for treating runny nose, as part of the herbal preparation Sinulan forte<sup>®</sup> was found to be effective in reducing rhinorrhea in patients with acute postviral rhinosinusitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be used to treat runny nose ailments, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid taking it with other medications, and be cautious when consuming it if you have a history of allergies or sensitive stomach.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Sambucus nigra tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with a low dose of 10-20 drops and increase as needed.
Use Sambucus nigra tea for 5-7 days to see noticeable results.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust the dosage or frequency as needed.
Combine Sambucus nigra tea with a warm compress for added relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of a runny nose. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce nasal congestion and combat underlying infections that can cause this condition. The bioactive constituents, including gingerols and shogaols, exhibit expectorant and mucolytic activities, facilitating the expulsion of mucus and relieving sinus pressure. Regular consumption of Zingiber officinale teas can provide relief from a runny nose, promoting a healthy respiratory system and overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of grated fresh ginger root.
Boil the water in a pot and add the grated ginger root.
Reduce heat and simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the ginger root.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and drink warm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of ethnopharmacology, Zingiber officinale teas have been traditionally used to cure various diseases including cold and respiratory disorders, suggesting that they may help alleviate symptoms associated with a runny nose.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can help alleviate the symptoms of a runny nose, but it may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, heartburn, and diarrhea due to its high ginger content. To use this herbal preparation safely, take it in moderation, especially with an empty stomach, and avoid consuming it before or after surgery as it may interfere with blood clotting.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor to confirm runny nose is not a symptom of an underlying condition.
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea, 3 times a day, for 3 days.
Add 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale powder to warm water or tea, 2 times a day.
Increase dosage to 2 cups of tea or 2 teaspoons of powder, if symptoms persist.
Monitor and adjust usage as directed by doctor or herbalist, for up to 7 days.

Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Reduce heat and add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the tea infuser.
Steep the flowers in the water for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to help alleviate runny nose symptoms, but possible side effects include drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat runny nose, it is recommended to use them in moderation and avoid taking them before driving or operating heavy machinery, as well as to monitor their consumption, especially in children and pregnant women.

Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia in the water for 5-7 minutes.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint teas, have been traditionally used to treat runny nose due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing nasal congestion, relieving sinus pressure, and promoting easy breathing. The bioactive constituents of peppermint teas, including menthol and menthone, contribute to their decongestant and expectorant effects, which help to clear mucus and reduce nasal discharge. The benefits of using peppermint teas to treat runny nose include natural relief from congestion, reduced sinus pressure, and a soothing effect on the nasal passages.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh mint leaves (Mentha x piperita) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 2-3 tablespoons of fresh mint leaves to a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the mint leaves.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day, as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of Allergy, Asthma & Immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Mentha x piperita teas, which is peppermint oil and leaf, may be used to treat symptoms of the common cold or upper respiratory tract allergies, which can include runny nose.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can help alleviate a runny nose due to their decongestant and antiviral properties, but potential side effects include stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness, particularly if consumed in large quantities or in combination with other medications. To safely use Mentha x piperita teas, it is recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming them on an empty stomach, and be cautious of interactions with other herbs or substances that may exacerbate digestive issues.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea pallida teas have been used for centuries to treat the runny nose ailment, also known as rhinorrhea, due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. These herbal preparations help to treat this ailment by reducing nasal congestion and excess mucus production, thereby alleviating symptoms such as a runny nose and sneezing. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea pallida teas, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and glycosides, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which contribute to the development of rhinorrhea. The benefits of using Echinacea pallida teas to treat the runny nose ailment include reduced symptoms, improved quality of life, and a natural alternative to conventional medications, making them a popular choice among those seeking holistic remedies.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea pallida flowers and leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the flowers and leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the flowers and leaves in the tea infuser or cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to 3 times a day as needed to help alleviate a runny nose.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Zeitschrift fur arztliche Fortbildung, Echinacea pallida teas may be beneficial for runny nose due to the significant pharmacological effects found in alcoholic extracts of its roots, which primarily target the nonspecific cellular immune system.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea pallida teas can be used to treat runny nose due to their immune-boosting properties, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as hives or itching, and interactions with certain medications that may affect blood sugar levels. To use Echinacea pallida teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase the amount to minimize stomach upset, and also to be aware of potential interactions with other herbal preparations or supplements, particularly those that affect blood pressure or liver function.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat the runny nose ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion and prevent infection. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe the nasal passages and reduce mucus production. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, Curcuma longa teas can help to alleviate symptoms of runny nose, such as nasal discharge and congestion, and promote a faster recovery. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat runny nose include reduced reliance on over-the-counter medications, fewer side effects, and a natural approach to maintaining respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Curcuma longa root and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot and add the dried Curcuma longa root.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate a runny nose.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Curcuma longa teas for runny nose are not explicitly mentioned, but it is mentioned that the bioactive compounds in Curcuma longa such as curcumin have anti-inflammatory and immunostimulatory properties, which could potentially aid in lowering the risk of severe COVID-19 symptoms, although there is no direct information on its effects on runny nose.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating a runny nose, but may cause potential side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize these risks, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming Curcuma longa teas if you have a history of stomach problems, are pregnant, or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, also known as wild marjoram, have been traditionally used to treat a runny nose due to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including carvacrol and thymol, help to reduce nasal congestion and relieve symptoms associated with a runny nose. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, these bioactive compounds assist in soothing the nasal passages and reducing the excessive mucus production. The benefits of Origanum vulgare teas in treating a runny nose include their natural, non-invasive, and easily accessible nature, making them a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to managing this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 2 tablespoons of dried Origanum vulgare leaves.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup to remove the leaves.
Add honey to taste, if desired, to make the tea sweeter.
Drink the tea hot or warm to help relieve a runny nose.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Origanum vulgare teas for runny nose have been found to have high antioxidant properties, with a Trolox equivalent antioxidant capacity (TEAC) of 5.87+/-0.2mM and a total phenolics content of 1653.61+/-11.52 microM, indicating potential benefits in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to help alleviate symptoms of a runny nose due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, but some people may experience side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, or allergic reactions like skin irritation or hives. Precautions should be taken when using Origanum vulgare teas, including starting with small doses, avoiding consumption before bed due to its potential to cause insomnia, and being cautious when using it with other medications or herbal remedies.
